always @ (cpu_fsm_state or start_toggle or cpu_ack_int)
    begin
        case (cpu_fsm_state)
            `STATE_IDLE:
            begin
                if(start_toggle && !cpu_ack_int) 
                    next_fsm_state <= `STATE_TRANSFER;  // Don't go to next state for 1-cycle transfer
                else 
                    next_fsm_state <= `STATE_IDLE;

          default:
                data_from_internal_reg = {{(32-NB_CORES){1'b0}}, internal_reg_status};
        endcase
    end

reset_facts.signal_or_condition: or word_count_zero or bit_count_max or data_register_i[52] or biu_ready or intreg_instruction or burst_instruction
reset_facts.text_preview:
always @ (module_state or module_next_state or module_select_i or update_dr_i or capture_dr_i or shift_dr_i or operation_in[2]
         or word_count_zero or bit_count_max or data_register_i[52] or biu_ready or intreg_instruction or burst_instruction
         or module_cmd or intreg_write or decremented_word_count)
     begin
    // Default everything to 0, keeps the case statement simple
    addr_sel <= 1'b1;  // Selects data for address_counter. 0 = data_register_i, 1 = incremented address count

bit_ct_en <= 1'b0;  // enable bit counter
    bit_ct_rst <= 1'b0;  // reset (zero) bit count register
    word_ct_sel <= 1'b1;  // Selects data for byte counter.  0 = data_register_i, 1 = decremented byte count
    word_ct_en <= 1'b0;   // Enable byte counter register
    out_reg_ld_en <= 1'b0;  // Enable parallel load of data_out_shift_reg
    out_reg_shift_en <= 1'b0;  // Enable shift of data_out_shift_reg

STATE_Rstatus:
        begin
           tdo_output_sel <= 2'h0;
           top_inhibit_o <= 1'b1;    // in case of early termination

word_ct_en <= 1'b1;
           bit_ct_rst <= 1'b1;  // Zero the bit count
           addr_ct_en <= 1'b1;  // Increment thte address counter
           top_inhibit_o <= 1'b1;    // in case of early termination
        end

// TAP states
				test_logic_reset_o,

// registers, but this uses binary encoding for the minimum of 4 DFF's instead.
`define STATE_test_logic_reset 4'hF
`define STATE_run_test_idle    4'hC
`define STATE_select_dr_scan   4'h7
`define STATE_capture_dr       4'h6
`define STATE_shift_dr         4'h2

end
		default: next_TAP_state = `STATE_test_logic_reset;  // can't actually happen
	endcase
end

        .td_i                    ( jtag_tdi_i             ),
        .td_o                    ( jtag_tdo_o             ),

.soc_tck_o               ( soc_tck_o              ),
        .soc_trstn_o             ( soc_trstn_o            ),
        .soc_tms_o               ( soc_tms_o              ),
        .soc_tdi_o               ( soc_tdi_o              ),
        .soc_tdo_i               ( axireg_tdo_i           ),

.test_clk_i              ( s_test_clk             ),
        .test_rstn_i             ( s_rstn_sync            ),

        .jtag_shift_dr_o         ( jtag_shift_dr_o        ),
        .jtag_update_dr_o        ( jtag_update_dr_o       ),
        .jtag_capture_dr_o       ( jtag_capture_dr_o      ),

`ifndef PULP_FPGA_EMUL

        .test_mode_i ( test_mode_o ),

asset_candidates.label: SoC memory/address space reachable through AXI master debug path
asset_candidates.rationale: Debug BIU/module exposes AXI master read/write address, data, and protection/user attributes, making downstream AXI-addressable memory/peripherals protected-asset candidates.

asset_candidates.label: Local interconnect/TCDM address space reachable through LINT debug path
asset_candidates.rationale: JTAG/LINT debug ports can issue local interconnect requests with address, write-enable, byte-enable, auxiliary attributes, and receive read data.

asset_candidates.label: CPU/core debug and OR1K control state
asset_candidates.rationale: OR1K debug modules include CPU selection and CPU bus/debug control signals, making core execution/control state a permission-sensitive target.

asset_candidates.label: JTAG TAP data/instruction/register state
asset_candidates.rationale: TAP and top-level debug scan/control state mediates access to debug modules and may expose or route protected data.

asset_candidates.label: APB debug/peripheral register space
asset_candidates.rationale: APB debug and peripheral bus instances are software/debug-visible register-access candidates.

asset_candidates.label: ROM/L2/SCM memory buses
asset_candidates.rationale: ROM and L2/instruction/data memory bus instances are protected memory-region candidates reachable through fabric/debug paths depending on integration.

asset_candidates.label: Debug transport FIFOs and synchronization registers
asset_candidates.rationale: FIFO/synchronizer state may hold data/control crossing debug and system clock domains.

asset_candidates.label: CRC/check state for debug transactions
asset_candidates.rationale: CRC logic supports transaction integrity/status in debug scan flows and may affect acceptance or reporting of debug transfers.

subject_candidates.label: External JTAG/debug requester
subject_candidates.rationale: JTAG pins and TAP state controls represent an external debug/test subject capable of driving scan commands.

subject_candidates.label: Advanced debug interface module selector
subject_candidates.rationale: Selected debug modules act as request issuers toward AXI, LINT, or CPU targets.

subject_candidates.label: AXI debug master
subject_candidates.rationale: The AXI BIU drives master-side transaction channels and is a requester on the system fabric.

subject_candidates.label: LINT/JTAG debug master
subject_candidates.rationale: The LINT debug path drives request/address/write controls onto local interconnect/TCDM buses.

subject_candidates.label: CPU debug requester/selected core
subject_candidates.rationale: OR1K BIU selects and controls CPU/core debug access paths.

subject_candidates.label: SoC internal bus masters adjacent to debug paths
subject_candidates.rationale: DMA and fabric-controller bus instances are requester candidates relevant for permission comparisons and mediation context.

operation_candidates.label: JTAG scan/capture/shift/update operation
operation_candidates.rationale: Scan operations load, shift, update, and expose debug data/control registers.

operation_candidates.label: AXI read operation from debug path
operation_candidates.rationale: Debug commands can be translated into AXI read address/data handshakes.

operation_candidates.label: AXI write operation from debug path
operation_candidates.rationale: Debug commands can be translated into AXI write address/data handshakes.

operation_candidates.label: LINT/local interconnect read operation from debug path
operation_candidates.rationale: Debug-to-LINT path has request/address/read-data signals for local interconnect reads.

operation_candidates.label: LINT/local interconnect write operation from debug path
operation_candidates.rationale: Debug-to-LINT path has request/address/write-enable/write-data/byte-enable signals for local writes.

operation_candidates.label: CPU/core debug access operation
operation_candidates.rationale: OR1K debug path can select cores and issue debug bus accesses/control operations.

operation_candidates.label: Expose read data through TDO/module TDO
operation_candidates.rationale: Readback data from bus/debug modules may be serialized to the external debug output.

operation_candidates.label: Reset/clear debug transaction state
operation_candidates.rationale: Reset and clear signals affect debug state machines, FIFO/sync state, and CRC state.

operation_candidates.label: Set route/module selection for debug access
operation_candidates.rationale: Module selection and inhibit controls determine which debug module/path is active.

guard_candidates.label: TAP/module selection mediation
guard_candidates.rationale: Selection and TAP state controls mediate when a debug module can capture/update/shift requests.

guard_candidates.label: Transaction valid/strobe and read/write qualification
guard_candidates.rationale: Strobe, read/write, ready/error, and word-size controls qualify debug bus operations.

guard_candidates.label: AXI fabric protocol and attribute mediation
guard_candidates.rationale: AXI protection/region/user attributes and responses are potential downstream permission/mediation signals.

guard_candidates.label: LINT grant/response mediation
guard_candidates.rationale: Grant, response valid/opcode/auxiliary information mediate or report local interconnect transactions.

guard_candidates.label: Core selection mediation
guard_candidates.rationale: CPU selection determines which core/debug target receives the operation.

guard_candidates.label: Reset/lifecycle gating
guard_candidates.rationale: Reset and clear conditions gate transaction state and may initialize/clear permission-relevant state.

guard_candidates.label: Clock-domain synchronization handshake
guard_candidates.rationale: Synchronization and FIFO handshakes mediate movement of requests/data across TCK and system/bus clock domains.

guard_candidates.label: Potential absent/implicit authentication or lifecycle authorization guard
guard_candidates.rationale: Given external JTAG/debug access to bus masters, an expected guard class is debug authorization/lifecycle enable; visible labels should track whether such signals exist and cover paths in later modules.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: TAP/debug reset initializes scan and module state
state_lifecycle_facts.rationale: TRST/reset input controls JTAG/debug-domain state initialization.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: System/bus reset initializes AXI/LINT/CPU BIU state
state_lifecycle_facts.rationale: Separate bus/CPU/system resets affect request FSMs and outstanding transaction state.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: Debug command update occurs on update_dr/capture/shift phases
state_lifecycle_facts.rationale: TAP phases load and commit command/data state used to launch debug transactions.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: Read data returns asynchronously to debug scan domain
state_lifecycle_facts.rationale: Returned bus data is staged for readiness and eventual scan-out, creating potential residual readback state.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: FIFO/synchronizer state can retain transaction bytes/control until reset or handshake completion
state_lifecycle_facts.rationale: Transport buffering and CDC registers may hold security-relevant debug data/control.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: CRC state reset/clear/update lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.rationale: CRC register evolves under enable/shift and clears/resets under clr/rstn.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: Debug route active/inhibited lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.rationale: Selection/inhibit outputs define active debug routing and top-level scan behavior.

path_candidates.label: External JTAG to AXI master transaction path
path_candidates.rationale: TAP scan/control inputs can select an AXI debug module that drives AXI master address/data/control and returns read data toward TDO.

path_candidates.label: External JTAG to LINT/local interconnect path
path_candidates.rationale: JTAG debug path can issue local interconnect requests and shift returned data outward.

path_candidates.label: External JTAG to CPU/core debug path
path_candidates.rationale: OR1K debug path routes scan commands to selected CPU/core debug bus controls.

path_candidates.label: LINT JTAG wrapper into SoC TCDM/fabric path
path_candidates.rationale: pulp_soc instantiates a lint_jtag wrapper and LINT JTAG bus alongside memory/fabric buses.

path_candidates.label: AXI debug master into system memory/peripheral map
path_candidates.rationale: AXI debug master outputs are candidate paths to any downstream AXI mapped asset.

path_candidates.label: Readback exposure path from bus response to TDO
path_candidates.source: axi_master_r_data or lint_r_rdata_i or CPU debug data
path_candidates.rationale: Data returned by bus/debug operations is likely staged into module output and serialized on the JTAG output.

path_candidates.label: Reset/clear path affecting debug availability and stale state
path_candidates.rationale: Multiple reset domains govern permission-relevant state and potential residual debug data.

path_candidates.label: SoC debug/APB bus adjacency path
path_candidates.rationale: Visible APB/debug and LINT debug bus instances indicate register/fabric paths relevant for permission mediation review.

summary: Semantic permission labels for visible RTL scope centered on advanced debug/JTAG interfaces bridging to AXI, LINT/local interconnect, OR1K CPU debug, APB/debug buses, memory buses, and SoC domains. Labels identify candidates only; no final vulnerability decision is made.
